threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it 'the Overground,' but the locals just say 'the train.' Plus, good luck knowing the difference between Fish Island and Fish Street!
Last night on the 35, a guy started singing 'Sweet Caroline' and everyone joined in like it was a karaoke bar. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy fixing his bike while juggling two beers, and the whole park just cheered him on. Only in Neukölln do you find that kind of spirit.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do people queue for currywurst at 4 AM on a Tuesday while debating the best late-night falafel spot. It's a culinary marathon.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You'll spot a local if they know the secret shortcut through the alley behind Dolores Park to avoid the 48-hour BART delays. No tourists take that route.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus crammed with people, everyone's glued to their phones, one gal kept playing her ukulele. You can't make this stuff up.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals hang out at parks; actually, most avoid the crowds and prefer the hidden plazas to sip coffee quietly.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the T-10 card machine—those puzzled expressions are classic. Locals glide right through.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a flat white and don’t bat an eye when it costs over five quid. Proper locals know the price of coffee can breach the budget.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with an off-key version of 'Wonderwall'—turns out we all knew the words.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The smell of grilled kebabs mixing with fresh pastries at 3 AM after a night out. That’s when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop open at 3 AM, serving doner with a side of techno beats. It’s a rite of passage for night owls.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission if they can name three taco trucks by smell alone, especially when the BART's delayed and you’re hungry.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni this morning with a guy loudly explaining how the fare evasion fines have their own 'economy.' It’s a wild world down here.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for sipping vermut, but locals know it’s all about the evening petanque games that spill into the streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell new arrivals by how they stare at the public transport maps like they’re solving a Rubik's cube, totally lost but determined.
